mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held above its recent trend while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets price persistent policy restraint and higher real yields, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day Treasury yield changes are not positive in at least 55% of periods with FEDFUNDS above its 6-month moving average and CPIAUCSL above its 12-month moving average, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE after a low-unemployment plateau signals labor-market deterioration and weaker growth expectations, which reduces expected policy tightening and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if yield changes are positive or flat in more than 60% of trigger events, or if the average 20-day yield change after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, consumer demand is outpacing housing supply conditions and supports stronger nominal growth expectations, lifting the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined condition RSAFS rising and HOUST falling does not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day yield changes are not positive in the majority of such joint episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock manufacturing recovery, rising INDPRO and stronger PAYEMS imply firmer real activity and reduced slack, which steepens nominal yield expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-and-PAYEMS signal fails to produce positive 60-day yield changes, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS move above 4.5% signals a restrictive policy regime in which front-end rates anchor longer maturities higher; the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 5 trading days as term premia reprice upward.
failure_condition: Reject if the average 5-day yield change after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% is not positive, or if the sign is negative in more than half of such observations.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS slows while UNRATE is already rising, the labor market is weakening on both employment and unemployment dimensions, increasing recession odds and lowering the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp in 2 months and PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration trigger is followed by positive or zero 20-day yield changes in most cases, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE and PAYEMS are combined.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS over several months indicates accelerating aggregate demand and firmer nominal growth, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day yield changes are not positive following simultaneous upward momentum in GDPC1 and RSAFS, or if the joint signal is weaker than the stronger single-series signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle slowdown, weak INDPRO together with rising UNRATE implies slackening real activity and lower expected inflation pressure, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield down within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 5-day yield response is not negative when INDPRO weakens and UNRATE rises together, or if the joint pattern fails to beat random baseline performance.
